fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Missouri Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Missouri - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity missouri, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to missouri, pharmacy technician compact missouri, missouri pharmacy technician endorsement application

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Missouri.

steps.title: Meet the Qualifications
steps.description: You must be at least 18 years old, have a high school diploma or equivalent, and be of good moral character.

steps.title: Complete the Application
steps.description: Submit a completed Pharmacy Technician License Application to the Missouri Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Pay the Fee
steps.description: Pay the $100 license fee to the Missouri Board of Pharmacy.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the PTCB. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Missouri pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Missouri have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Missouri. To work in Missouri, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Missouri?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Missouri while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Missouri
hero.dek: Missouri Pharmacy Technician licensure requires completing an application, paying the required fee, and meeting the board's qualifications.
